sections.paragraph:
προσϰυνέω [πρός, ϰυνέω ‘to kiss’ (freq. part of social ritual)] ‘recognize another’s prestige by offering special honor’, ordinarily through a gesture of prostration, do obeisance (to), pay homage (to)

—a. to humans Mt 18:26; Ac 10:25; Rv 3:9. Especially in honor of Jesus Mt 2:2, 8, 11; 9:18; 14:33; 15:25; J 9:38.

—b. to beings considered transcendent (whether identified as such expressly or by implication), ordinarily w. focus on religious aspect, worship

—α. deity in monotheistic cult Mt 4:10; Lk 4:8; J 4:20f, 22b; Hb 1:6; 11:21; Rv 4:10; 5:14.

—β. deity in polytheistic cult Ac 7:43.

—γ. malevolent beings Mt 4:9; Lk 4:7; Rv 9:20 and freq. in Rv.

—δ. angelic beings, w. gesture of prostration tantamount to religious devotion 22:8.

—ε. Jesus as risen Lord Mt 28:9, 17; Lk 24:52.
